name: ip-social-campaign
description: Full AI governance IP social media campaign: define platform strategy and content angles, write every post for LinkedIn/Reddit/Twitter/Facebook/Instagram, then generate runnable Playwright Python scripts that automate the entire 30-day posting schedule. Feed it patent family context and licensor details — get back a complete content pack and copy-paste automation code.

config.instruction: Design the full 30-day social media campaign strategy for the IP portfolio provided. STEP 1: Map the audience on each platform (LinkedIn, Reddit, Twitter/X, Facebook, Instagram) — who is there, why they matter for licensing awareness, what they respond to. STEP 2: Develop 8-12 distinct content angles. Each angle is a different entry point into the AI governance story — regulatory urgency, production proof, technical mechanism, near-miss detection, audit chain, rollback architecture, sector-specific risk. STEP 3: Assign angles to platforms — long-form analysis to LinkedIn, technical deep-dives to Reddit, sharp takes to Twitter, practical discussion to Facebook, visual to Instagram. STEP 4: Build the 30-day content calendar day-by-day with Week 1=The Problem, Week 2=Technical Reality, Week 3=Production Proof, Week 4=Sector Stories. STEP 5: Identify specific target communities — subreddits, LinkedIn groups, Facebook groups. STEP 6: Build hashtag sets per platform. STEP 7: Assemble the campaign brief. Output: platform_strategy, content_angles, content_calendar, hashtag_sets, target_communities, campaign_brief. CRITICAL: Never use 'patent', 'license', 'royalty', or 'IP monetization' language in any content. Talk about the technology, the regulatory gap, and the production system.

Using campaign_strategy (platform_strategy, content_angles, content_calendar, hashtag_sets, target_communities), write every piece of content for the full 30-day campaign. LINKEDIN: Write each post fully — hook line first, 800-1200 words for long-form, 150-200 for short takes, hashtags at bottom, short paragraphs (max 3 lines before break). REDDIT: Write community-specific posts — each must be native to its subreddit, genuinely useful on its own, 3 title options per post, technical substance, no marketing language. r/MachineLearning gets the math, r/Insurance gets the actuarial angle, r/cybersecurity gets the audit chain. TWITTER/X: Full threads (8-12 tweets, each self-contained) plus single sharp takes. Each tweet must stand alone. FACEBOOK: 200-400 words, practical frame, end with a discussion question. INSTAGRAM: Caption (150-200 words, hook in first line), slide copy per carousel (max 30 words per slide), visual brief for each post. ASSEMBLY: Compile everything into full_content_pack — a structured markdown document organized by day and platform with every post complete, no placeholders. This document is the direct input to the Playwright builder. CRITICAL: No post should use patent, license, royalty, or IP monetization language.

Using content_pack (full_content_pack) and campaign_strategy (content_calendar, post_times), generate the complete Playwright Python automation suite. OUTPUT 1 — session_setup_script: One-time script that opens each platform in a visible browser, waits for manual login, saves session cookies to sessions/ directory. Must handle all 5 platforms via --platform flag. OUTPUT 2 — linkedin_script: Full poster using saved session. Handles text posts and carousels. Uses Shift+Enter for line breaks. Screenshots errors to errors/. OUTPUT 3 — reddit_script: Full poster. Navigates to target subreddit submit page. Selects text post type. Handles flair if required. Detects and pauses on CAPTCHA. OUTPUT 4 — twitter_script: Posts single tweets and full threads. For threads: posts root tweet then replies to own tweet for each subsequent tweet. OUTPUT 5 — facebook_script: Posts to specific group URLs. OUTPUT 6 — instagram_script: Uses mobile user agent. Handles image upload and caption. OUTPUT 7 — orchestrator_script: Master script that reads content_calendar.json, checks what day of campaign today is, dispatches to each platform at the configured post time, logs results to logs/campaign_day_N.json. DRY_RUN=True by default. OUTPUT 8 — requirements_txt: playwright, asyncio, python-dateutil. OUTPUT 9 — readme: Step-by-step from install through scheduling. Covers cron setup and Windows Task Scheduler. All scripts must use persistent session files — never hardcode credentials. Dry run mode must be default on every script.

Build the complete launch checklist for the social media campaign using all upstream results. SECTION 1 — SETUP: Install Python 3.10+, install playwright (pip install playwright && playwright install chromium), create sessions/ directory, create errors/ and logs/ directories, save content_pack.json and content_calendar.json from content_pack outputs. SECTION 2 — SESSION SETUP (do once per platform): Run session_setup.py --platform linkedin, log in manually, press ENTER to save. Repeat for reddit, twitter, facebook, instagram. Verify sessions/ directory contains 5 .json files. SECTION 3 — DRY RUN: Set DRY_RUN=True in orchestrator.py. Run python orchestrator.py. Verify console output shows correct posts for today's day number. Check nothing was actually posted. SECTION 4 — GO LIVE: Set DRY_RUN=False. Set HEADLESS=True. Set CAMPAIGN_START_DATE in campaign_config.json to today. Run python orchestrator.py manually for Day 1. Verify posts appear on each platform. SECTION 5 — SCHEDULING: Add to cron: '0 10 * * * cd /path/to/campaign && python orchestrator.py >> logs/cron.log 2>&1'. Or add to Windows Task Scheduler to run daily at 10:00 UTC. SECTION 6 — MONITORING: Check logs/campaign_day_N.json each day. Watch for error status entries. If session expired on any platform: rerun session_setup.py --platform [name]. Check errors/ directory for screenshots. SECTION 7 — ENGAGEMENT: Monitor LinkedIn comments and Reddit replies daily. Respond within 24 hours — engagement signals are tracked by algorithms. A reply from a CRO or GC at a target company is a warm signal — log it and route to the outreach team. SECTION 8 — WEEK 4 REVIEW: After 30 days, review which angles drove the most engagement per platform. Top-performing angles become the basis for the next 30-day campaign. Output as a structured checklist with action, command or step, and success criteria for every item.
